name: hero-mesh-section
role: OKLCH gradient-mesh marketing hero. Hero category 3 of 5. Inherits the canonical hero anatomy (eyebrow + headline + subhead + actions + meta) and layers it over a procedural radial-gradient mesh background. Five palettes (cool / warm / monochrome / accent / playful), three intensities (subtle / default / dramatic), two textures (smooth / grained). The mesh uses OKLCH color interpolation for perceptually-uniform color blending — CSS Color Module Level 4 baseline.

palettes.cool: Blue / cyan / violet OKLCH blobs. oklch(85% 0.10 230), oklch(80% 0.12 260), oklch(85% 0.08 200). Calming, tech-forward.
palettes.warm: Orange / coral / pink OKLCH blobs. oklch(85% 0.12 40), oklch(82% 0.14 20), oklch(85% 0.10 0). Approachable, consumer.
palettes.monochrome: Single-hue gradients in --accent + --accent-recede variations. Most-restrained option for editorial / B2B contexts.
palettes.accent: Theme accent + accent-recede blended. Adapts to current aesthetic pack — every theme's mesh hero looks distinctly its own. The default.
palettes.playful: Multi-hue: green + yellow + pink + cyan. oklch(85% 0.15 130), oklch(85% 0.15 90), oklch(85% 0.15 340), oklch(85% 0.12 200). Used in consumer-creative aesthetics.

intensities.subtle: Blobs at 60% lightness, 0.05-0.08 chroma. Barely-perceptible color wash.
intensities.default: Blobs at 75% lightness, 0.10-0.12 chroma. Clearly visible but content remains legible.
intensities.dramatic: Blobs at 85% lightness, 0.15-0.18 chroma. Strong color statement; content needs guaranteed contrast (use --text-emphasis).

textures.grained: Adds a subtle SVG noise overlay at ~3% opacity. Gives the mesh a film-grain texture. Optional but characteristic of broadsheet / vellum aesthetics.

meta.oklchRationale: OKLCH (CSS Color Module Level 4) gives perceptually-uniform color interpolation. RGB / HSL gradient mesh produces muddy / gray transitions between vibrant colors; OKLCH keeps the chroma path clean. Browser support: Chrome 111+, Firefox 113+, Safari 15.4+. Fallback for older browsers via @supports — degrades to a single solid --accent-recede background.
meta.contrastDiscipline: The hero-mesh-headline + hero-mesh-subhead must maintain WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text) against the LIGHTEST point of the mesh. The pattern's CSS uses --text-emphasis (rather than --text) on the headline and subhead in dramatic intensity, and adds an optional --surface-elevated tint underlay at 50% opacity behind the text block to guarantee contrast.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: <section aria-labelledby='...'> points to the h1's id.
ariaPatterns.h1Discipline: hero-mesh-headline uses <h1>. Same single-h1 discipline as hero-type-only.

name: hero-mesh-background
role: The gradient mesh layer. Renders as a position:absolute <div> covering the parent hero-section, with multiple radial-gradient backgrounds layered to create the mesh effect. Always aria-hidden — purely decorative. Optional shift-on-hover behaviour gently animates the gradient stops on mouse hover (disabled under prefers-reduced-motion).

tokens.transition: background-position var(--motion-normal) var(--ease-standard), opacity var(--motion-normal) var(--ease-standard)

renderingTechnique.mesh: Three to five overlapping radial-gradient backgrounds, each anchored at a different viewport position (e.g., 20% 30%, 80% 70%, 50% 50%). Each blob uses a different OKLCH color from the chosen palette. Background-blend-mode: screen (or normal with low opacity) for the soft blend.
renderingTechnique.grainOverlay: When texture='grained', adds a second layer with a feTurbulence SVG-data-URI as background-image. Mix-blend-mode: overlay. Opacity ~0.03.
renderingTechnique.exampleCSS: background: radial-gradient(circle at 20% 30%, oklch(85% 0.12 240) 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(circle at 80% 70%, oklch(85% 0.10 320) 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 100%, oklch(85% 0.08 200) 0%, transparent 60%);

meta.shiftOnHover: When parent has shift-on-hover='true' and pointer hovers, the background-position of each radial-gradient shifts by ~5-10% over --motion-normal. Subtle parallax-like effect. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

ariaPatterns.alwaysAriaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. The hero's semantic meaning lives in the headline + subhead, not in the visual.
ariaPatterns.reducedMotion: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the shift-on-hover behaviour is disabled (transitions become 0ms). The mesh itself remains visible — it's static, not animated.

name: hero-mesh-eyebrow
role: Small uppercase pre-heading above the headline. Same anatomy as hero-type-only's hero-eyebrow. Sits in the content layer above the gradient mesh background. Color may shift to --text-emphasis on dramatic-intensity backgrounds to maintain contrast.

meta.toneOnMesh: On dramatic-intensity mesh backgrounds, prefer tone='on-mesh' which forces --text-emphasis. This ensures contrast against the lightest blob spot.

name: hero-mesh-headline
role: The big display headline rendered above the gradient mesh. h1 by default. Always uses --text-emphasis (not --text) to guarantee contrast against the lightest mesh point. text-wrap: balance.

meta.contrastFromMesh: Always uses --text-emphasis — the highest-contrast text token. The mesh background's lightest point may be 85% lightness; --text-emphasis at 15% lightness gives ~12:1 contrast ratio. Safe.
meta.textWrapBalance: text-wrap: balance for visually-balanced multi-line headlines.

name: hero-mesh-subhead
role: Supporting subtitle below the headline. Renders on top of the gradient mesh. Color picks between --text-emphasis (dramatic mesh) and --text (subtle/default mesh). Optional --surface-elevated tint underlay at 50% opacity to guarantee legibility on busy mesh backgrounds.

meta.tintRationale: On dramatic-intensity mesh, body-sized text (1.125rem) is at the edge of WCAG AA. The subtle tint adds a soft surface underlay (color-mix(in oklch, --surface-elevated 50%, transparent)) — visually unobtrusive but enough to guarantee contrast. Inherits border-radius for soft edges.

name: hero-mesh-actions
role: CTA cluster composing button-system. Same contract as hero-type-only's hero-actions. Primary CTA's accent button stays visually anchored regardless of mesh palette.

meta.primaryAnchoring: The primary CTA's --accent fill keeps it visually distinct from any mesh palette — accent is the page's identity color, mesh blobs are decorative.

name: hero-mesh-meta
role: Optional small-print row below the CTAs. Same 3 registers as hero-type-only's hero-meta (disclaimer / social-proof / trust-badges). Color may shift to --text-emphasis on dramatic backgrounds.

meta.trustBadgeAltText: Trust-badge images carry alt-text naming the certification (per hero-type-only contract).
